aiExtractionChallenges: Content split into frames (corpsclc.html + corpsclw.html), Hot Potatoes JS-generated inputs/feedback, and relative frame navigation make single-file extraction and ordering difficult.
aiScriptRisk: The frameset file has no scripts itself, but the framed pages likely contain Hot Potatoes scripts — stripping scripts would remove input fields, correct answers, validation and feedback UI/logic.
aiMissingContent: Main cloze content (corpsclc.html — questions/text and input fields), feedback/solutions (corpsclw.html), Hot Potatoes-generated interactive UI/answers, and the "Viyiz étout" see‑also links (now empty).

aiPageType: interactive drag-and-drop matching quiz (Hot Potatoes activity).
aiExtractionChallenges: Much content is produced at runtime via JavaScript (document.write, arrays F/D and beginDrag handlers), uses absolute-positioned card elements and CDATA blocks plus ISO-8859-1 encoding, so static HTML extraction misses generated items and layout.
aiScriptRisk: High — the generated card/choice text (arrays F and D), the dynamic divs for draggable cards (F0.., D0..), feedback, scoring and control UI would be lost.
aiMissingContent: All draggable card contents / matching pairs (arrays F and D), the dynamic card divs and their onmousedown behavior, feedback messages and controls; "Viyiz étout" list entries are empty and scripts appear escaped/garbled.
